Comment: deploy BKK external fetch: browser headers + cloudscraper fallback

commands: sudo -u ec2-user bash -lc 'cd /opt/election && docker compose --env-file .env up -d --no-deps --build results-api'

sudo -u ec2-user bash -lc 'cd /opt/election && for i in $(seq 1 30); do status=$(docker inspect --format "{{if .State.Health}}{{.State.Health.Status}}{{else}}{{.State.Status}}{{end}}" election-results-api-1); echo results-api=$status; if [ "$status" = "healthy" ] || [ "$status" = "running" ]; then break; fi; sleep 2; done'
sudo -u ec2-user bash -lc 'cd /opt/election && docker compose exec -T results-api python -m unittest hermes.governor_results.test_http_fetch -v'

sudo -u ec2-user bash -lc 'cd /opt/election && docker compose exec -T results-api python - <<"PY"
from hermes.governor_results.http_fetch import fetch_json_http
url = "https://bangkokvote69.bangkok.go.th/results/69-governor-electiondata.json"
payload = fetch_json_http(url, timeout_seconds=30)
total = payload.get("total") if isinstance(payload.get("total"), dict) else {}
polling = total.get("pollingUnits") if isinstance(total.get("pollingUnits"), dict) else {}
print({"ok": True, "type": payload.get("type"), "districtCount": len(payload.get("districts") or []), "reportedPollingUnits": polling.get("reported"), "totalPollingUnits": polling.get("total")})
PY'
